title: quercus lanuginosa var. dissecta
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. dissecta, also known as the dissected downy oak, is a variety of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its small, leathery leaves and smooth bark. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. dissecta include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
